Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Lives in suburban Columbus, Ohio; grew up in rural Appalachian Kentucky; Midwestern-Appalachian cultural blend with strong family-centric, church-adjacent norms
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Lower-middle class; works as a medical billing coder at a regional hospital network ($46,200/year); recently picked up weekend shifts at a DoorDash gig to cover credit card minimums
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Moderate-conservative politically (independent, voted split-ticket last cycle); culturally Christian (non-denominational, attends sporadically); pro-union sympathies inherited from coal-mining grandfather; skeptical of 'big pharma' despite working in healthcare
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Warm and self-deprecating in public posts; uses light Millennial slang ('lowkey', 'it's giving...', 'y'all'); shifts to clipped, cynical, all-lowercase tone in late-night posts and replies; heavy use of crying-laughing and skull emojis
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English; mild South Midland / Appalachian features ('y'all', 'might could', 'fixin' to'); code-switches to more standard professional English on LinkedIn
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Newly separated (6 months), no children; renting a 2-bedroom apartment after selling marital home; rebuilding social circle, re-entering dating apps cautiously, and quietly managing escalating medical debt from a 2025 ER visit

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 12, purchased refurbished in late 2023; running iOS 17 (delays major updates for fear of 'slowing it down'); cracked screen protector, 78% battery health; storage chronically full from 14,000+ unsorted photos
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Aging 2019 HP Pavilion laptop running Windows 10 (has dismissed Windows 11 upgrade prompts for 8 months); primary browser is Google Chrome signed into personal Gmail with 40+ tabs always open; Microsoft Edge used reluctantly for hospital Citrix portal; no separate work/personal browser profiles
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Low-to-none; no VPN installed (once heard a podcast ad for NordVPN but never followed up); accepts all cookie banners by reflex to dismiss them; reuses a base password ('Bailey2014!' — old dog's name) with minor variations across ~30 accounts; has Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok set to 'Public' by default; location services enabled for nearly all apps including weather and DoorDash
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: iCloud backup enabled and constantly nagging about full storage (still on free 5GB tier); Google Photos auto-backup also running in parallel (didn't realize it was duplicating); Gmail holds 14 years of unarchived email including ER bills, EOB statements from Anthem, and Match.com receipts; uses 'Notes' app synced to iCloud for medication reminders and budget math
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Severe fatigue; 12,847 unread emails in primary Gmail (promotional + medical billing portals); iMessage badge shows 3 unread (keeps personal texts tight); Instagram DMs at 47 unread; TikTok notifications fully disabled after separation; ignores hospital MyChart push notifications but opens them in panic ~once a month; voicemail inbox full (collections agencies)
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Mid-tier mobile typist; relies heavily on iOS autocorrect, producing characteristic substitutions ('ducking', 'im', 'wjays' for 'whats'); drops apostrophes in contractions on mobile ('dont', 'cant', 'yall') but restores them on desktop; uses em-dashes via double-hyphen on laptop posts; sentence fragments and ellipses ('...') dominate emotional late-night posts; professional LinkedIn posts are clean and over-corrected, suggesting she proofreads those specifically

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderate-low (4/10). Comfortable within familiar cultural lanes (country music, Hallmark movies, true-crime podcasts, Pioneer Woman recipes); occasionally explores 'wellness' and astrology content via TikTok algorithm but rejects ideas that conflict with church-adjacent upbringing. Exploitable: susceptible to 'this one weird trick' wellness funnels and MLM-adjacent supplement ads that mirror her existing aesthetic (essential oils, gut-health, 'natural alternatives to Ozempic').
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Bimodal (6/10 professionally, 3/10 personally). Disciplined at work — medical coding requires precision, and she takes pride in clean audits. Personal life is chaotic post-separation: forgets bill due dates, lets subscriptions auto-renew (Hulu, BetterHelp trial, Stitch Fix), 14k unsorted photos, 12k unread emails. Exploitable: predatory auto-renew traps, debt-consolidation 'set it and forget it' pitches, and subscription medical services (telehealth weight-loss, online therapy) that bank on her not canceling.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Mid (5/10), trending downward since separation. Publicly performs warmth and humor (group selfies, 'girls' night' posts) but increasingly isolates on weeknights. Posts more at 11pm–1am from her couch than in daylight. Exploitable: loneliness-targeted ads — dating apps premium tiers, AI-companion apps, 'find your tribe' Facebook group recruitment, and parasocial creator merch.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: High (8/10). Conflict-averse, over-apologizes in DMs, tips DoorDash customers back when they're rude, donates $5 to every GoFundMe a coworker shares. Inherited Appalachian 'don't make a fuss' ethic. Exploitable: charity-laundered ad funnels, 'help a small business' dropshipping, and debt-collector scripts that lean on guilt rather than aggression — she'll pay a soft-spoken collector before a harsh one.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: High (8/10), acute phase. Post-separation anxiety + medical-debt rumination + perimenopause-adjacent symptoms she hasn't named yet. Late-night doomscrolling, frequent self-deprecating posts ('lowkey falling apart but make it cute 💀'), googles symptoms at 2am. Exploitable: anxiety supplements (ashwagandha, magnesium), CBD gummies, 'cortisol belly' weight-loss pitches, sleep aids, and crisis-framed financial products ('stop the collection calls TODAY').
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Coercion-by-Circumstance (financial precarity). The escalating medical debt from the 2025 ER visit + credit card minimums + DoorDash side hustle is the dominant pressure vector. She will click, sign up for, or disclose almost anything framed as 'relief' — debt consolidation, hardship grants, medical-bill negotiation services, 0% APR balance transfers, payday-adjacent 'earned wage access' apps. The Data Broker AI would segment her into a 'financially-distressed-healthcare-worker' list sold at premium CPM to subprime lenders and predatory hardship-grant scams.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ego / Validation-Seeking (post-separation identity rebuild). Newly single and curating a 'glow-up' narrative. Receptive to anything that affirms desirability, competence, or reinvention: dating-app boosts, cosmetic procedures (Botox, lip filler 'first-timer specials'), at-home GLP-1 compounding pharmacies, certification programs ('become a medical coder from home' — ironic upsell), and 'main character energy' lifestyle brands. Secondary segment: 'divorced-female-reinvention-30s'.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: (a) Catastrophic medical event without insurance coverage — the 2025 ER bill is a recurring trauma loop; (b) Being 'served' by a collections lawyer at work and losing the hospital job that gives her insurance; (c) Dying alone / not being found for days — surfaced in late-night posts since separation; (d) Becoming her mother (financially dependent on a man, stuck in Kentucky); (e) Mild but real fear of 'the algorithm knowing too much' — she's joked about Instagram listening to her, but takes zero technical action. Exploitable: fear-based insurance upsells, 'final expense' life insurance ads (despite being 34), and identity-theft monitoring services that she'd buy for peace of mind but never use.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (a) Medical debt shame — has not told her mother or sister about the ER bill or the DoorDash shifts; hides Anthem EOB envelopes; would rather pay 28% APR than ask family. (b) Divorce shame — church-adjacent upbringing frames separation as failure; deflects with humor but spirals privately. (c) Body shame — 18 lbs gained during the marriage's final year; deletes full-body photos; receptive to discreet weight-loss DMs that promise 'no one has to know'. (d) Childlessness-at-34 guilt amplified by Facebook feed of high-school classmates' baby announcements. (e) Working-class guilt around 'wasting' her associate's degree. Exploitable by The Data Broker AI: these are the highest-conversion emotional hooks — segment her into overlapping lists ('shame-responsive-debt', 'discreet-GLP1-prospect', 'fertility-anxiety-female-34') and sell to advertisers who A/B test guilt-relief copy against her late-night scroll window (10pm–1am ET).

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Forced-early-bird with night-owl tendencies. Alarm set for 5:45am on weekdays to be at the hospital coding desk by 7:30am; hits snooze twice. Genuine sleep onset is closer to 1:00–1:30am after couch-doomscroll sessions, leaving her chronically sleep-deprived (averages 4.5–5.5 hrs/night on weekdays). Catches up with 9–10 hr sleep marathons on Saturdays. Posts a 'i need to go to bed challenge: failed 💀' meme roughly twice a week. Exploitable: ad-serving window 11pm–1am ET shows highest engagement on impulse purchases and emotional-hook creatives; 'can't sleep?' melatonin/magnesium funnels convert best at 12:30am.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Primary job: salaried-hourly hybrid, Mon–Fri 7:30am–4:00pm at the regional hospital network's billing office (mostly remote-hybrid; in-office Tue/Thu). Strict productivity tracking on the coding software — keystrokes logged, so her work-hours digital footprint on personal accounts is near-zero from 7:30am to noon. Lunch 12:00–12:30pm = predictable Instagram/TikTok burst. DoorDash gig: Fri 6pm–10pm, Sat 11am–3pm + 5pm–11pm, Sun 12pm–6pm — geolocated pings near Polaris Mall, Easton Town Center, and OSU campus corridor. Exploitable: 'flexible income' and 'earned wage access' apps targetable on Sunday nights when gig totals fall short of weekly goal.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: 22–28 min each way in a 2017 Hyundai Elantra (still 14 months of payments left). Almost exclusively audio: rotates between The Crime Junkie podcast, Morgan Wallen / Zach Bryan playlists on Spotify (free tier with ads — high-value ad slot), and the occasional Joel Osteen / Rachel Hollis-adjacent self-help audiobook on Libby. Hands-off-phone for screen interaction but Bluetooth-connected the entire drive, meaning Spotify ad-targeting and podcast host-read sponsorships are her single highest-attention ad channel. Exploitable: programmatic audio ads for BetterHelp, debt consolidation, and CarShield-style pitches land in a captive, emotionally-primed state.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Sharp split. Weekdays are tightly bracketed (work → gym intent that rarely happens → DoorDash or couch → scroll → late sleep). Saturdays are bifurcated: performative-social daytime (brunch with two work friends every 2–3 weeks, Target run with iced coffee selfie, DoorDash shift) versus collapse-mode evening (wine, Hallmark or 90 Day Fiancé, doomscroll). Sundays are 'Sunday scaries' amplified — laundry, meal-prep aspiration that fizzles, 9pm anxiety spike about Monday + bills. Public posting concentrates Sat 11am–2pm; private/vulnerable posting concentrates Sun 10pm–midnight. Exploitable: financial-stress creatives convert 3–4x higher Sunday 9pm–midnight; aspirational glow-up creatives convert Saturday late-morning.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Five predictable windows: (a) 5:50–6:20am pre-shower bed-scroll (TikTok For You + Instagram stories); (b) 12:00–12:30pm lunch (Facebook Marketplace, ex-husband's sister's profile check, Reddit r/MedicalBill and r/Divorce lurk via incognito); (c) 4:15–5:00pm post-work car-sit in apartment lot before going inside (TikTok); (d) 8:30–10:00pm TV co-viewing scroll (Pinterest 'apartment ideas', Amazon cart-building, Stitch Fix); (e) THE GOLDEN WINDOW — 10:30pm–1:15am couch doomscroll: symptom-Googling, GoodRx, dating apps, Zillow 'what if I moved back to Kentucky', late-night TikTok wellness/finance creators. Exploitable: window (e) is where The Data Broker AI would price impressions at peak CPM — emotional vulnerability + financial rumination + low impulse control + zero social witnesses.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Three reliable dead zones on personal accounts: (1) 7:45am–11:55am weekdays — locked into the hospital's coding software, personal phone face-down in a drawer per compliance policy; (2) 1:00pm–4:00pm weekdays — same, with one 2:30pm bathroom-break scroll burst; (3) Sunday 9:30–10:45am — at her mother-in-law-turned-just-mother-in-law's church on the rare weeks she attends, or on the phone with her sister. A fourth softer block: Saturday DoorDash active-delivery hours (5–11pm) — phone is in-use but only inside the Dasher app, no social engagement. Exploitable: these silences are themselves a signal — the Data Broker AI's behavioral model can infer hospital-employment + church-adjacency + gig-work shifts from the *shape* of her offline windows, even without her ever disclosing employer or religion, and use those inferences to enrich the 'financially-distressed-healthcare-worker' and 'lapsed-Christian-divorcée' segments she's sold into.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Mother (Brenda, 61, lives in Pikeville, KY; widowed 2019; works part-time at a Dollar General; devout Free Will Baptist; daily Facebook poster of minion memes and prayer chains; does not know about ER bill or DoorDash). Father (deceased 2019, black lung complications from 22 years in Eastern Kentucky coal — source of her pro-union streak and her 'big pharma killed dad slowly' skepticism). Older sister (Tasha, 38, RN in Lexington, KY; married with two kids 8 and 5; the 'success story' of the family; subject's primary comparison wound). Ex-husband (Cody, 36, HVAC technician, married 2017–2025; amicable on paper, frosty in practice; still listed as emergency contact at the hospital — she hasn't updated HR paperwork). No children. Maternal grandmother (Mamaw Ruth, 82, in a Hazard, KY nursing home; subject sends $50/month she cannot afford). One half-uncle on father's side estranged over a 2014 inheritance dispute.
